LEATT GROWTH OS

A Microsoft Fabric-powered ecommerce intelligence platform — from the real Leatt storefront through a Lakehouse/Data Factory engine, a live Power BI report, ML return-risk scoring, and SAP-ready financial reconciliation.

R3.99bnnet revenue
48.5%gross margin
2,925,594units sold
4.9%return leakage
2,000,000modeled transaction lines

The story

Leatt sells motocross and adventure safety gear — helmets, body protection, boots, gloves, goggles. The business question behind this platform: is growth profitable growth? R3.99bn in modeled net revenue carries a 48.5% gross margin and 4.9% return leakage — Apparel is the largest category (R1.76bn), and Organic Search the leading acquisition channel (R998.0m).

The Fabric engine — what's real, and how data actually moves

A capacity, workspace, Lakehouse and Power BI report were created via the real Fabric REST APIs — these are genuine, live items in the tenant, not a diagram of them:

Workspace Apollis   e515bafe-7290-4832-ae1d-514be43a9d87
Lakehouse Leatt_BI_ML_Lakehouse   dca60749-eaef-410e-9121-ea16eedbc975
Power BI report Leatt BI ML Executive Report   8c6988a7-fe5e-4fbe-abe9-ce7edd7fd63e
Capacity leattfabricf2 (SKU F2, South Africa North) — Paused when not in active use

How the data actually moved

The physical files below were pushed into OneLake by a Python script (src/fabric/upload_to_onelake.py) authenticating with an Azure CLI token and calling OneLake's storage REST API directly (create-directory, append, flush) — this is the real, verified transfer mechanism, confirmed by matching byte counts on both ends.

LayerFileSizePurpose
Bronzeleatt_ecommerce_transactions_2m.parquet90.7 MB2,000,000-row transaction fact
Bronzeleatt_product_catalog.csv8.9 MB11,354 Shopify product variants
Bronzeleatt_synthetic_customers.csv13.5 MB180,000 synthetic customers
Silverleatt_customer_ml_scores.csv31.0 MBCustomer propensity/value ML scores
A Fabric Data Factory pipeline item (pl_leatt_million_row_lakehouse_load, 9e82c185-e0ac-485d-9810-4bccdcfe6cf9) is genuinely registered in the workspace via the Fabric API — proving the integration exists — but it was not configured with copy activities and run; the physical move above happened via the script. A separate, fully designed pipeline template (src/fabric/fabric_data_factory_pipeline_template.json) specifies what a production build would do: a Copy activity pulling Leatt's live Shopify product API straight into Bronze, a Copy activity landing the transaction parquet into Bronze, and a Notebook activity building the Silver Delta table — a real design, presented as a design, not as something proven to have executed.

Semantic model tables

TableTypeGrainRowsDescription
fact_transaction_linesFactOne ecommerce order line2,000,000Core sales, discount, revenue, margin, return, payment and campaign fact.
dim_customerDimensionOne synthetic customer180,000Customer geography, rider type, acquisition channel, loyalty tier, age band and opt-in status.
dim_product_variantDimensionOne Shopify product variant11,354Extracted Leatt product/variant catalog with category, SKU, price, availability and source URL.
dim_dateDimensionOne calendar date912Calendar/date attributes derived from order_date for time intelligence.
customer_ml_scoresML OutputOne customer score180,000Repurchase propensity, value score, margin rate, return rate and ML segment.
agg_monthly/category/product/province/channelGold AggregatesReporting aggregateSmallPower BI-ready aggregate tables for executive dashboards.
finance_reconciliationFinance MartOne accounting period30VAT, refunds, sales excl/incl VAT, payment fees and expected cash reconciliation.
Honest caveat: the Power BI report was created and bound to the semantic model via the real Fabric API (operation succeeded, 100% complete), but an automated service export only produced loading/sign-in screens rather than rendered visuals — those blank exports are deliberately not presented as visual proof here. The report is real and reachable at its live URL; visual screenshots require a signed-in interactive session.

The money map

Growth quality, not just growth: category concentration, channel mix and the margin trend across 30 months of modeled trading.

Issues, root causes & prevention

A KPI dashboard that only reports numbers is half a BI platform. The other half is a structured way to ask "why", decide what to do, and catch the next problem before it costs money. This section walks that loop end to end using the real signals generated from the gold layer — nothing here is a generic best-practice list, every reading below is a live number.

Root-cause diagnostic playbook

When a headline number moves, this is the decision tree used to find out why before reacting:

ProblemDiagnostic branchQuestion to askLikely interpretation
Revenue downCategory demandWhich categories moved versus 3-month average?Campaign issue, product availability, seasonality or assortment gap.
Revenue downChannel mixDid traffic/revenue shift away from efficient channels?Media allocation, SEO ranking or landing-page relevance issue.
Margin downDiscountingDid discount depth increase by category/channel?Promo overuse or weak pricing control.
Margin downReturnsAre returns rising in a specific category/product?Sizing, content, fulfillment or quality leakage.
Returns upProduct fitAre high-risk items clustered by category, size or product line?Improve size guide, PDP detail and customer education.
Returns upFulfillmentAre return rates linked to fulfillment days or province?Delivery promise or regional service gap.
ROAS downTraffic qualityIs revenue falling while spend remains high?Creative fatigue, wrong audience, poor landing page.
Audit exceptionData lineageDo source/target row counts reconcile?Duplicate, missing or stale pipeline load.

Decision signals monitored

Each signal has a threshold rule and an assigned owner — this is what makes the dashboard operational rather than decorative.

SignalReadingHealthDecision
Revenue momentumR3,994,247,413Healthy scaleTrigger exception commentary in the monthly trading pack.
Margin quality48.5%Strong but must be protectedProtect margin-positive product mix and bundle high-margin items.
Return leakage4.9%WatchlistImprove sizing, delivery promise, PDP content and post-purchase guidance.
Hero category concentrationApparel R1,759,430,045Strategic dependencyCreate availability alerts and category-specific SEO/story pages.
Channel efficiencyDirect 100.0x ROASScale carefullyMove spend toward efficient journeys; keep paid experiments controlled.
Data completeness2,000,000 rowsAudit-readyPublish source, target, row count and commit ID in every close pack.
Cloud costFabric F2 pausedControlledResume only for refresh/demo, then pause again.

Prevention layer 1 — ML return-risk scoring

A logistic regression model (AUC 0.59, recall 71.8%) scores every transaction line for return risk. The high-risk watchlist sample flags 25,000 lines worth R48.6m at risk, concentrated in Apparel — a ranked list to act on before the return happens.

Prevention layer 2 — forecast & anomaly detection

A 6-month forward revenue forecast (next month: R129.6m) combined with a monthly anomaly score lets the business see the Nov/Dec margin dip coming rather than explaining it after the fact — the same months flagged by the anomaly score are exactly the months with real margin compression.

Next best actions

OwnerActionRationalePriority
CEOProtect hero categoryMake Apparel the board-level growth theme; it contributes R1,759,430,045 revenue.High
CMOScale efficient acquisitionUse Direct as always-on efficiency engine with ROAS 100.0x.High
COOReduce return leakageInvestigate Footwear return-rate drivers before increasing media spend.Medium
MerchandisingBuild margin bundleBundle Jacket ADV FlowTour 5.5 - Women with compatible accessories and protection products.High
Data GovernanceOperationalize evidencePublish source register, row counts, file hashes, capacity status and refresh runbook into monthly close pack.High
BI OpsInvestigate revenue anomaly2025-12 changed 12.6% versus 3-month average.Medium

Prioritized initiatives (ranked by value × confidence, effort-adjusted)

ScoreInitiativeEst. valueConfidenceEffortNext step
92Return leakage reduction sprintR19,750,642MediumMediumCreate return-reason taxonomy and product-level return dashboard.
90Hero category availability and SEO programR12,808,359HighMediumBuild category scorecard with stockout, ranking, margin and landing page signals.
88Margin-positive bundle testingR19,384,973MediumLowRun A/B test: product page bundle versus normal recommendation carousel.
84Channel budget guardrailsR7,753,989MediumLowSet campaign guardrails: minimum ROAS, conversion lift and margin contribution.
82SAP close reconciliation automationRisk reduction / faster closeHighMediumSchedule monthly exception pack with owner, SLA and SAP account mapping.

Prevention layer 3 — data controls (stopping bad data before it reaches the board)

AreaControlDescriptionOwnerFrequency
CompletenessDaily row count controlCompare ecommerce lines, invoices, payments and refunds by day/monthFinance OpsDaily
AccuracyVAT recalculationRecompute VAT at 15% and compare to source tax amount within toleranceRevenue AccountantDaily
ValiditySAP document existenceEvery BI order/invoice should map to a SAP document number after postingSAP AnalystDaily
ConsistencySKU and GL mappingEvery product category maps to valid SAP item group, revenue account, COGS account and VAT codeData StewardWeekly
TimelinessPipeline SLAFabric/ADF loads must finish before finance close cut-offBI EngineerDaily
ReconciliationSales-to-bank controlSales incl. VAT less refunds/fees equals payment settlement within timing toleranceFinance OpsDaily
GovernancePII minimizationHash/anonymize customer identifiers for analytics where detailed identity is not neededData OwnerMonthly
AuditabilityImmutable evidencePersist source files, run logs, row counts, hashes and exception registerData Governance LeadMonthly close

Competitive positioning

CompetitorPositioningLeatt opportunity
Fox RacingMX and MTB gear leaderLeatt should lean harder into safety science, protection credibility, and South African origin story.
AlpinestarsPremium motorsport protectionCompete with test evidence, fit guides, and protection-first product storytelling.
100%Performance eyewear and gogglesFor Leatt eyewear, emphasize lens protection, riding conditions, and bundle with helmets.
OakleyPremium sport opticsLeatt must avoid pure optics battle; position eyewear as protection plus ride-specific value.
SCOTTBike and moto equipmentUse comparison content: Leatt vs Scott by lens, fit, helmet compatibility, price.

Ecommerce revenue is reconciled to VAT, refunds and expected cash — the layer that lets Finance trust the BI numbers enough to post them.
